title: murraya kwangsiensis
intro: Murraya kwangsiensis, also known as the orange jasmine or mountain orange, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to southern China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for reducing stress, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Murraya kwangsiensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
